items.title: France's parliament adopts assisted dying law
items.description: France on Wednesday moved closer to joining the ranks of countries that guarantee the right to assisted dying, a move championed by President Emmanuel Macron. Macron had promised an assisted dying law when he was re-elected for a second term in 2022, in a change seen as one of the most important social reforms since France allowed same-sex marriage in 2012. If the country's highest constitutional authority approves the legislation, France will join the Netherlands, Belgium, Switzerland and Canada in legalising assisted dying.

items.title: Wagner's disinformation network across Africa: France denies asylum to insider turned whistleblower
items.description: For Spotlight, François Picard is pleased to welcome Lea Perruchon, Journalist with Forbidden Stories. She exposes a collision of competing interests: journalism, France's national security and their complex asylum policy. Ephrem Yalike-Ngonzo, a former participant in Russia's disinformation campaign in the Central African Republic, turned whistleblower, offered crucial testimony that helped uncover the operations of the Wagner-linked disinformation network across Africa. Following an intervention reportedly supported by the French presidency to evacuate him and his family from imminent danger, French authorities changed course and abruptly rejected his asylum application.

items.title: 'You cannot have a good assisted dying law without good palliative care'

France's long awaited assisted dying legislation marks a profound shift in the country's approach to end of life care, but as Reynaud makes clear, the parliamentary vote is less the end of a debate than the beginning of a new ethical and legal era. items.description: The lower house of France's parliament adopted bill for assisted dying. But the the government bypassed the right-wing dominated Senate and the law will instead go to the highest constitutional court for final approval.

items.title: Sinner beats Zverev to retain Wimbledon title
items.description: Jannik Sinner retained his Wimbledon title by beating Alexander Zverev in Sunday's men's final. The Italian claims his fifth Grand Slam crown and extends his dominance over the German to 10 straight victories.

items.title: Argentina and England renew fierce World Cup rivalry in semi-final
items.description: Lionel Scaloni's side defeated Switzerland 3-1 to book a spot in the World Cup 2026 semi-finals, setting up a sixth meeting with England – and their first at the tournament in 24 years. Leer más

items.title: EU approves new passenger rights for air travel
items.description: EU ministers have approved a set of reforms giving more rights to air travelers. Among other things, compensation claims are to be made easier.

items.title: Senior Chinese delegation visits North Korea for talks
items.description: China's fourth-highest-ranked official, Wang Huning, held talks with a top official of North Korea's governing party.

items.title: China economic growth falls sharply, missing target
items.description: Weak demand domestically and the impact of the Iran war on oil prices overshadowed the country's strong exports.

items.title: Report finds Brisbane has country's best nightlife — if it's Thursday
items.description: The Queensland capital outranks every other city for Thursday nightlife trade, a report finds.

items.title: India-U.K. DCC not applicable to Indians working in the U.K. before July 15
items.description: DCC came into effect on July 15, 2026 alongside the India-U.K. Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA)

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: England vs. Argentina: A football rivalry full of history
items.description: From the Falkland Islands to Diego Maradona and David Beckham, there is so much history between the two 2026 World Cup semifinalists.

items.title: Starmer says political journey 'over' as he faces last question session
items.description: Outgoing UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer, who announced his resignation last month, has taken part in his last weekly grilling from MPs in parliament.

items.title: Argentina's World Cup semi-final hero Lautaro Martínez 'dreamt' of scoring winner

items.title: More than 500 feared dead as officials investigate reports of boats capsizing in recent weeks
items.description: UN agencies say more than 500 people are feared dead after reports that two boats carrying members of Myanmar’s persecuted Rohingya minority have capsized in the Bay of Bengal

items.title: Senegal government silences Football Federation amid World Cup row
items.description: As tensions are escalating following Senegal's exit from the World Cup, the Senegalese government has stepped in and ordered the country's Football Federation to refrain from giving interviews or making public statements until further notice.

items.title: U.S. House of Representative passes bill to ‘ditch the switch’ and make daylight saving time permanent
items.description: Daylight saving time is that period between spring and fall when clocks in most parts of the United States are set one hour ahead of standard time

items.title: Ukraine: Kyiv and EU agree on new drone production deal
items.description: Ukraine and the EU have agreed on a drone deal combining Kyiv’s know-how with the bloc's scale-up power. Meanwhile, Russian attacks caused more fatalities in Odesa as Moscow increasingly targets the port.

items.title: U.S. approves nearly $2 billion in weapons sales to Saudi Arabia
items.description: This proposed sale will support the foreign policy and national security objectives of the United States by improving the security of a Major non-NATO Ally that is a force for political stability and economic progress in the Gulf Region, says U.S. State Department

items.title: Family of Australian missing in US says somebody 'does know something'
items.description: Australian police say they're ready to help US authorities, as a weeks-long search for Melbourne woman Sally Grace Contarino continues in Las Vegas.

items.title: Why is Zelenskyy reshuffling Ukraine's government?
items.description: Ukraine's Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko has resigned, prompting a Cabinet reshuffle led by President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. What comes next?

items.title: Italy's Meloni suffers surprise setback in close vote on electoral reform
items.description: The defeat triggered opposition calls for Meloni to resign ahead of next year's general election.

items.title: Donald Trump set to feature on gold coin to celebrate 250 years of U.S. Independence
items.description: The 24-karat gold coin, portraying Mr. Trump leaning on the Resolute desk with clenched fists, will be only the second time a living President has featured on a coin

items.title: Hundreds face homelessness as accommodation centre set to close
items.description: Hundreds of residents fear they could be left homeless after the Northern Territory government announced it will shut a Darwin short-stay accommodation centre in less than 90 days.

items.title: Iran displays new banner showing Trump lying in casket in central Tehran
items.description: This comes as Iran's supreme leader on Saturday vowed revenge for the US-Israeli killing of his father and predecessor, hours after President Donald Trump warned against any attempt to assassinate him.

items.title: 13th Kinshasa International Film Festival opens
items.description: Born out of a belief that Africans should watch African films and tell their own stories, the Kinshasa International Film Festival opened its doors for its 13th year in the Congolese capital

items.title: Sierra Leone drops treason charge against ex-president over attempted coup
items.description: Ernest Bai Koroma was arrested in 2023 after gunmen broke into an armoury and some jails, freeing inmates.

items.description: Russians have long pulled the strings when it comes to the governing body of international chess. But now two Germans are vying for FIDE's top job.

items.title: World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight

items.title: Makoko waterfront reclamation in Lagos approved by FG, state govt – Official
items.description: The official maintained that the reclamation project near the Third Mainland Bridge underwent environmental assessments, secured all required federal and state approvals, and was cleared by the relevant regulatory agencies before work resumed. items.title: Argentina’s Falklands banner sparks controversy at World Cup

Germany's Ruhr Valley: Cities on brink of financial collapse
items.description: With tax revenues down and social spending up, more and more cities and towns in the Ruhrgebiet are struggling. Oberhausen is particularly deep in the red.

items.description: Police said they found a "military-grade weapon" during the search of a suspicious car parked in a Paris neighborhood with a large Jewish population.

items.title: Celebrations as Gibraltar-Spain border fence removed

items.title: Activists fear China's ethnic unity law could silence critics in Australia

items.title: Trump says Iran has freed a detained U.S. citizen
items.description: Dena Karari’s attorney said what drew Tehran authorities to Ms. Karari was her operation of the non-profit Children of Mehr Foundation, which helped impoverished children in Iran through private donations.
